title: Japanese Stir-Fry with Gooey Sauce
description: A plant-based Japanese stir-fry dish featuring gooey sauce, perfect for a quick lunch or dinner. You'll need some basic pantry staples and a few simple ingredients.

ingredients.name: Sour Gooey Sauce (vegan)
ingredients.notes: You can find this in most Asian grocery stores or make your own by mixing together 2 tablespoons of rice vinegar, 1 tablespoon of soy sauce, 1 tablespoon of maple syrup, and 1 tablespoon of cornstarch

ingredients.name: Tofu
ingredients.notes: Drain and press the excess liquid

ingredients.name: Plant-based stir-fry mix
ingredients.notes: You can also use a combination of broccoli, carrots, and bell peppers

ingredients.name: Sesame oil
ingredients.notes: You can also use vegetable oil

instructions: 1. In a small bowl, whisk together the gooey sauce and 2 tablespoons of water. Set aside.
2. Heat the sesame oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
3. Add the tofu and cook until golden brown, about 3-4 minutes on each side.
4. Add the stir-fry mix and cook until tender, about 3-4 minutes.
5. Add the ginger and cook for 1 minute.
6. Pour in the gooey sauce and stir to combine.
7. Add the green onions and sesame seeds (if using) and stir to combine.
8. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the sauce has thickened.
9. Serve immediately over rice or noodles.
10. Garnish with additional green onions and sesame seeds (if desired)
